entries.what_they_read: Headline: 'Trade player tokens that move with real stats.' Then the three-step onboarding and the fair-value vs market-price mechanic. Skimmed the LeBron $5.59 example hard.
entries.their_interpretation: It's a fantasy sports AMM on devnet — stats oracle sets fair value, market price drifts, you fade the gap. Basically a spread trade on NBA player tokens with fake SOL.
entries.their_rationale: No wallet, no seed phrase, no signup — zero friction. Devnet SOL so no real exposure. The fair-value vs market-price mechanic is legit edge framing; I want to see the actual spreads on the cards before I decide if this is worth time.
confusion_notes: 'oracle updates' — how frequent? That's the whole timing question for holds, not explained
All tickers showing 0.0% — is this a dead market or just pre-open? Makes me wonder if there's actually anyone to trade against
'0.667 SOL · $100' — why show SOL denomination if it's devnet? Slightly noisy
Badge unlock condition 'hold through 3 oracle updates' — no cadence given, could be 3 days or 3 weeks
first_impression_elements: Live ticker scrolling player tokens with prices — eye went there first, scanned for spread/volatility, saw all 0.0% and flagged it
Big 'Claim $100 →' button — clean CTA, no friction copy next to it
LeBron $5.59 fair-value example — concrete number made the mechanic click instantly
ignored_elements: Badge descriptions beyond the unlock conditions
The 'New here? 10-second version' plain-language explainer block — didn't need it

checkpoint.q1_what_am_i_buying: Fake token tied to a player — LeBron, KD, whoever. Play money, devnet SOL, no real skin. It's a position on whether the market's mispriced him relative to what his stats say he's worth.
checkpoint.q2_what_moves_price: Two things — oracle pushes a new fair-value when stats update, and every buy/sell from other users nudges the market price directly. Big players like LeBron barely move on a single trade, role players rip harder. I'm watching both vectors.
checkpoint.q3_hold_week: Honestly not 100% sure on the cadence — app says 'oracle updates' happen but doesn't tell me exactly when or how many per week. If his stats stay strong the fair-value should tick up, and if market catches up I'm in profit. Diamond Hands badge needs 3 oracle updates held, so there's that angle too.
checkpoint.q4_fair_vs_market: Fair value is the computer's number — pure stats-to-price conversion, updated on some schedule. Market price is what the last trade actually cleared at. Gap between them is the edge — UNDERVALUED means market's sleeping, OVERVALUED means hype's run ahead of the numbers. I'm flipping that spread.
